sections.title: After completing The Forsaken Tower quest

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Thank you for helping to repair the Doors of Dinh. Kourend is a safer place because of you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Who are you?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: I'm Councillor Unkar, one of the members of the Kourend Council. We are responsible for running the entire Kingdom of Great Kourend.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What brings you to Lovakengj then?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: It's important for the council to maintain a good relationship with the rulers of the five houses. Although it is the council that rules, we can only do so with their support.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: I'm here to help Lady Lovakengj out and to make sure she has everything she needs to rule effectively.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: What are the Doors of Dinh?

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: They are a great set of doors built by the legendary smith, Dinh, in the early days of Kourend. They were created to imprison a deadly beast known as the Wintertodt.

lines.speaker: Councillor Unkar
lines.text: Unfortunately, the doors were recently damaged, putting Kourend at risk. The master smith Undor, managed to repair them though, something he couldn't have done without you.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'll get going.

sections.title: After completing the A Kingdom Divided quest

lines.speaker: Kubec Unkar
lines.text: Human.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: How's the cell?

lines.speaker: Kubec Unkar
lines.text: A step down from what I'm used to.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Well you'd better get used to it. This is where you'll spend the rest of your life, however long that ends up being.

lines.speaker: Kubec Unkar
lines.text: Oh, I'm all too aware of that. Here, I will fade into obscurity as the world forgets my actions. I've accepted it. Besides, it's for the best that I not be remembered.

lines.speaker: Kubec Unkar
lines.text: I only hope that the work I did for my home endures. Everything I did was to ensure that Lovakengj prospered.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: I'm sure they'll be just fine. If anything, I imagine they're better off without you.

lines.speaker: Kubec Unkar
lines.text: Don't be so sure. I've no doubt the new king has good intent, but our intentions mean little compared to our actions.

lines.speaker: Player
lines.text: Something you're all too aware of, I'm sure.

lines.speaker: Kubec Unkar
lines.text: Indeed. But I guess only time will tell. Farewell human.
